rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place component type contracts in component type annexes and implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes
Use only visible identifiers from the current component type or implementation scope
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the appropriate scope to maintain architectural integrity
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope to ensure valid references
Preserve the original AADL structure when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Avoid mixing component type and implementation-level bindings in the same annex block
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use valid AGREE syntax for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Ensure all referenced identifiers are visible in the current scope
anti_patterns: Do not insert AGREE annex blocks in invalid scopes that would disrupt the AADL architecture
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless properly represented
Do not mix component type and implementation-level bindings in the same annex block
Ensure all referenced identifiers are visible in the current scope to avoid unresolved references
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
AADL property names are not AGREE variables
